feedbackConfig.title: Feedback Configuration

feedbackConfig.scopeAll: Entire organization — anyone can send to anyone
feedbackConfig.scopeDepartment: Same department only — feedback between area colleagues
feedbackConfig.scopeTeam: Direct team + department — manager, reports and colleagues
feedbackConfig.scopeNote: Administrators can always send to any employee regardless of this setting.
feedbackConfig.allowAnonymousDesc: Employees can hide their name when sending feedback.
feedbackConfig.allowPeerDesc: Employees at the same hierarchical level can send feedback to each other.
feedbackConfig.requireCompetencyDesc: Require selecting a competency from the catalog when sending feedback.

evaluaciones.title: Evaluation Cycles
evaluaciones.subtitle: Evaluations assigned to you
evaluaciones.noCompletedYet: You have not completed any evaluations yet
evaluaciones.createFirstCycle: Create your first cycle to get started

guide.title: User Guide: Evaluation Cycles
guide.whatIsDesc: A cycle groups performance evaluations for a period. You configure the type (90°, 180°, 270°, 360°), assign evaluators and process the results.
guide.type90: 90°: Only the manager evaluates the employee
guide.stagesAutomatic: When creating a cycle, stages are generated automatically based on the type
guide.stagesComplete: Each stage must be completed before advancing to the next
guide.stagesFlow: Stages: Self-evaluation → Manager Evaluation → Peer Evaluation → Calibration → Feedback Delivery → Closure
guide.anonymityConfig: In the cycle configuration you can define which evaluator types are anonymous
guide.anonymityDefault: Default: peers and externals are anonymous, manager and self-evaluation are visible
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: Creates cycles, assigns evaluators, advances stages, closes
guide.permManager: Team Lead: Views team assignments, completes evaluations
guide.permEmployee: Employee: Completes self-evaluation, views results at closure

nuevo.title: New evaluation cycle
nuevo.subtitle: Configure a new cycle in 4 steps
nuevo.basicInfoDesc: Define the name and description of the evaluation cycle
nuevo.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the purpose of this evaluation cycle...
nuevo.cycleConfigDesc: Select the evaluation type and date range
nuevo.type90Detail: 90° — Manager only: The direct manager evaluates the employee. The simplest type. No self-evaluation or peer evaluation. Ideal for quick evaluations or first cycles.
nuevo.type180Detail: 180° — Manager + Self-evaluation: The employee self-evaluates and is also evaluated by their direct manager. Promotes personal reflection and alignment of perceptions.
nuevo.type270Detail: 270° — Manager + Self + Peers: Includes 180° plus evaluation by team peers. Offers a more complete view of performance considering colleague perspectives.
nuevo.type360Detail: 360° — Complete evaluation: Includes 270° plus a calibration stage and direct feedback delivery to the employee. The most complete process, recommended for annual performance reviews.
nuevo.selectTemplateDesc: Choose the question template for this cycle
nuevo.noTemplates: No templates available. Create a template first.
nuevo.reviewDesc: Confirm the data before creating the cycle
nuevo.createError: Error creating the cycle. Please try again.

detail.saveError: Error saving changes. Check dates and try again.
detail.autoGenDetail: Self-evaluations, direct manager evaluations and direct reports will be created as applicable. For 270° cycles, same-department peers are assigned automatically (min. 3). For 360° cycles, peers must be added manually.
detail.launchDetail: Evaluations will be sent to all participants.
detail.closeDetail: No more evaluations can be submitted.
detail.assignDesc: Configure who evaluates whom before launching the cycle
detail.autoGenExceptionsDetail: The following assignments could not be completed automatically. Review and resolve manually from the assignments panel.
detail.noAssignments: No evaluators assigned yet. Use "Generate Assignments" or add manually before launching the cycle.
detail.noSuggestions: No suggestions available for this employee.
detail.guideManage: What can I do here? Manage an evaluation cycle — view its status, manage evaluator assignments, launch or close the cycle.
detail.guideStates: Cycle states: Draft (assignments can be edited), Active (evaluators complete forms), Paused (temporarily stopped), Closed (final results available).
detail.guideAutoAssign: Automatic assignments: The "Generate assignments" button automatically creates evaluations based on the cycle type.
detail.guidePeers: Peer assignment: For 270° and 360° cycles, you must add peer evaluators manually or use "Suggest peers".
detail.guideLaunch: Launch: When launching the cycle, email notifications are sent to all evaluators.
detail.guidePermissions: Permissions: Only administrators can manage cycles and assignments.

calibracion.title: Evaluation Calibration
calibracion.subtitle: Collaborative sessions to review and validate performance scores
calibracion.loadError: Could not load sessions. Please try reloading the page.
calibracion.createError: Error creating the session. Please try again.
calibracion.noSessionsHint: Create a session linked to a completed cycle to get started

guide.title: Guide: Evaluation Calibration
guide.whatIsDesc: A process where the leadership committee reviews and adjusts evaluation scores to ensure fairness and consistency across teams. Primarily used in 360° evaluations.
guide.whenUsedDesc: After all cycle evaluations are completed, before delivering results. It is a stage of the evaluation cycle activated in 360° evaluations.
guide.flowStep1: Create session linked to a closed cycle or calibration stage
guide.flowStep3: Review scores and make justified adjustments
guide.flowStep4: Adjustments greater than 1 point require written justification
guide.flowStep5: Complete the session — calibrated scores are applied to the Nine Box Matrix
guide.connCycles: Evaluation Cycles: calibration is a stage of the 360° cycle
guide.connTalent: Talent (Nine Box): calibrated results feed the talent matrix
guide.connReports: Reports: calibrated scores are reflected in analytics
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: Creates sessions, adjusts scores, closes calibration
guide.permManager: Team Manager: Can participate as reviewer in their department's sessions
guide.permEmployee: Employee: Does not have access to this feature

form.title: New calibration session
form.subtitle: Complete the details to create the session. Fields marked with * are required.
form.name: Name *
form.notesPlaceholder: Observations or context for the session...

calibracionDetalle.emptyState: No participants loaded in this session.

distribution.deviatedAlert: ⚠ Deviated distribution — actual distribution does not match the expected curve.
distribution.howToReadDesc: Bars show how many employees scored in each range. The yellow line is the theoretical normal curve. If bars closely follow that line, the distribution is balanced.

guide.title: Guide: Objectives
guide.desc: Objectives let you define measurable goals for each employee, aligned with the organization's strategy. They can be linked to evaluation cycles to measure completion in performance reviews.
guide.draftNote: Objectives you propose will remain in 'Draft' status until your manager approves them.
guide.typeOkrDesc: Define what you want to achieve (the objective) and how you'll know you achieved it (key results).
guide.typeKpiDesc: A number measured continuously to know if work is on track. Updated periodically and useful for detecting trends.
guide.typeSmartDesc: A specific, measurable, achievable, relevant and time-bound objective.
guide.flow: Flow: Create / Propose → Draft → Approve → In progress → Complete

talento.colPerformanceHint: Automatically calculated from the average of completed evaluations
talento.colPotentialHint: Score 0–10 entered manually by admin/manager. Click a row to edit.
talento.colReadinessHint: Promotion readiness — entered manually by admin/manager
talento.colFlightRiskHint: Employee turnover risk — entered manually by admin/manager

talento.segReadinessHint: Promotion readiness assigned in the Nine Box tab
talento.segFlightRiskHint: Flight risk assigned in the Nine Box tab
talento.matrixAxisNote: ↑ Y-Axis: Performance (based on evaluations). → X-Axis: Potential (assessed manually by manager/admin).

guide.title: How does Talent Management work?
guide.nineBoxDesc: Classifies employees in a 3×3 matrix crossing Performance (vertical axis) and Potential (horizontal axis). Admins can edit Potential, Readiness and Flight Risk directly in the table.
guide.segmentationDesc: Flat view of all classified employees, with search and pool filters. Useful for finding all 'Stars' without knowing which quadrant they are in.
guide.dataSourceTitle: Where does the data come from?
guide.dataPerformance: Performance: automatically calculated from the average of completed evaluations
guide.dataReadiness: Readiness: manually entered (Ready now / In 1 year / In 2 years / Not ready)

analytics.title: Cycle Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Statistical distribution, competency map and team performance

guide.title: Guide: Cycle Analytics
guide.desc: This page presents organizational statistics from evaluation results per cycle. Select a cycle to view metrics. Data comes from completed evaluations.
guide.chartBell: Score Distribution (Bell Curve): Histogram with superimposed normal curve. Shows whether distribution is balanced or biased.
guide.chartDept: Department Comparison: Average score per department.
guide.chartHeatmap: Heat map: Department × template section matrix. Green = high, yellow = medium, red = low.
guide.chartManagers: Manager ranking ordered by average score of their team members.
guide.connReports: Collaborator Reports: For individual analysis, go to the Reports section
guide.connCalibration: Calibration: If calibration was performed, adjusted scores are reflected here
guide.connTalent: Talent (Nine Box): Performance scores seen here feed the Nine Box performance axis
guide.permissions: Permissions: Only Administrators and Team Managers can access this page.

analytics.howToReadDesc: Bars show how many employees scored in each range. The yellow line is the theoretical normal curve. If bars closely follow that line, the distribution is balanced.
analytics.noDataCurve: Insufficient data for the distribution curve
analytics.minDataRequired: At least 5 evaluations are required to display the distribution
analytics.alertLeniency: ⚠️ More than 60% of evaluations fell in the high zone. Possible leniency bias — consider calibration.
analytics.alertHarshness: ⚠️ More than 60% of evaluations fell in the low zone. Possible severity bias or widespread performance issues.
analytics.alertCentral: ⚠️ High concentration in the middle range. Low differentiation — the scale may not be used effectively.
analytics.dispersionLow: Very low dispersion: reviewers tend to assign very similar scores.
analytics.dispersionHigh: High dispersion: there is a lot of variability between scores.
analytics.dispersionNormal: Normal dispersion: variability is healthy and allows good differentiation of performance levels.

postulantes.title: Applicant Evaluation
postulantes.subtitle: Evaluation processes for external and internal candidates

guide.title: Applicant Evaluation Guide
guide.whatIsDesc: The Applicant Evaluation module manages selection processes for internal and external candidates. Each process is linked to a specific position and uses the organization's competency model to evaluate candidates objectively and systematically. Multiple evaluators can independently rate each candidate, and the system automatically calculates average scores to facilitate comparison.
guide.flowStep1: The administrator creates an evaluation process specifying the position and department. Department managers are automatically assigned as evaluators, and guest evaluators from other areas can be added.
guide.flowStep2: Candidates are added to the process (new external applicants or existing internal employees).
guide.flowStep3: Each evaluator accesses the Scorecard tab and rates the candidate on each position competency (1-10 scale).
guide.flowStep4: The system automatically calculates the final score as the average of all evaluations received.
guide.flowStep5: The Comparative tab displays a matrix of all candidates vs competencies, with gap indicators against expected levels.
guide.flowStep6: The administrator updates each candidate's status based on the result: Approved, Rejected or Hired.
guide.rule1: Each candidate can only be added once per process (duplicate control).
guide.rule2: Evaluations use a 1 to 10 scale, with one score per competency per evaluator.
guide.rule3: The final score is automatically recalculated each time an evaluator submits or updates their evaluation.
guide.rule4: For internal candidates, the system displays additional talent pool data: performance, potential and Nine-Box position.
guide.rule5: Evaluated competencies correspond to those configured for the position in the Development module (Role Competencies).
guide.statusAppliedDesc: The candidate was added to the process but has not been evaluated yet.
guide.statusApprovedDesc: The candidate passed the evaluation process successfully.
guide.statusRejectedDesc: The candidate did not meet the process criteria.
guide.statusHiredDesc: The candidate was selected and hired for the position.
guide.connCompetencies: Role Competencies: competencies and expected levels come from the Development module.
guide.connTalent: Talent: for internal candidates, their performance evaluation, potential and Nine-Box position are displayed.
guide.connUsers: Users: internal candidates are linked to existing employees in the system.
guide.connAi: AI Analysis: for external candidates, CVs are analyzed with AI to evaluate role fit, strengths and areas for development.
guide.connEvaluations: Evaluations: for internal candidates, the complete performance evaluation history is displayed.
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: creates processes, adds candidates, assigns evaluators, changes statuses and configures processes.
guide.permManager: Manager / Evaluator: evaluates candidates in assigned processes, views scorecards and comparatives.

desarrollo.title: Development Plans
desarrollo.subtitle: Management of individual development plans (IDP) for your team
desarrollo.createFormSubtitle: Define an IDP with concrete actions linked to competencies

guide.title: How do Development Plans work?
guide.whatIsDesc: IDPs define concrete actions for each employee's professional growth. Each plan is composed of specific actions (courses, mentoring, projects, workshops, readings or rotations) linked to competencies from the organizational catalog.
guide.flowStep1: Create plan (Draft) — Administrator or Manager creates an IDP for an employee
guide.flowStep2: Activate plan — Confirmed and the employee can see their assigned actions
guide.flowStep3: Execute actions — The employee completes courses, projects, mentoring, etc.
guide.flowStep4: Complete plan — Only the Administrator or Manager marks the plan as finished
guide.permAdmin: Administrator / Manager: Create plans, add actions, activate and complete
guide.permEmployee: Employee: Views their plan, completes individual actions, adds comments

actions.title: Development Actions
actions.empty: No development actions. Add at least one action.

orgDesarrollo.subtitle: Organization-level development initiatives

orgDesarrollo.subtitleManager: View the organization's development plans and initiatives
orgDesarrollo.employeeRestricted: This section is only available for administrators and managers.

firmas.signDesc: A verification code will be sent to your email to confirm your signature on the following document:
firmas.enterCodeDesc: Check your email and enter the 6-digit code you received.
firmas.signedDesc: Your digital signature has been recorded with SHA-256 hash, timestamp and IP.
firmas.subtitleAdmin: Manage and verify all digital signatures in the organization
firmas.subtitleManager: Your digital signature history and your team's
firmas.noResults: No results for the applied filters
firmas.guideProcessText: The system sends an OTP code to the signer's email. The signer enters the code to validate their identity. A SHA-256 hash of the document is generated as evidence.
firmas.guideValidityText: Each signature records: who signed, when, from which IP, and the document hash at the time of signing. If the document is modified afterwards, the hash will not match.
firmas.guideStatesText: Valid (unaltered document), Revoked (signature annulled by administrator).
firmas.guidePermAdmin: As an administrator you can view all signatures in the organization and verify their integrity.
firmas.guidePermManager: As a team manager you can view your signatures and those of your team members.
firmas.guidePermEmployee: You can view the history of your own digital signatures.

dei.title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)
dei.subtitle: Organizational composition metrics and evaluation equity analysis
dei.loadError: Error loading DEI data. Verify you have administrator permissions.
dei.noAlerts: No bias alerts detected for this cycle.
dei.emptyState: No active users. Add employees to see DEI metrics.
dei.configDesc: Configure thresholds that determine when bias alerts are generated and the minimum people to show group metrics.
dei.actionPlaceholder: Describe the action to address the identified gap...

guide.title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Guide
guide.whatIsDesc: The DEI module monitors the organization's demographic composition and detects bias in performance evaluations. It analyzes gaps by gender, seniority, age, tenure, department and nationality, generating alerts when differences exceed configured thresholds.
guide.section1: Summary cards: total employees, genders, nationalities and age ranges represented.
guide.section2: Demographic distributions: composition bars for each dimension.
guide.section3: Data completeness: percentage of demographic fields filled by employees.
guide.section4: Equity analysis: select an evaluation cycle to see bias alerts and dimension gaps.
guide.section5: Corrective actions: register and track actions taken to address identified gaps.
guide.rule1: Groups with fewer than the configured minimum (default: 10) are hidden to protect privacy.
guide.rule2: Bias alerts are visible only to administrators and HRBP.
guide.rule3: Alert thresholds are configurable per organization.
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: views all metrics, configures thresholds, registers corrective actions.
guide.permManager: Manager: views demographic metrics and equity alerts for their organization.

surveys.title: Climate Surveys
surveys.subtitle: Measure your team's engagement and satisfaction
surveys.createFirst: Create your first climate survey to get started
surveys.needOneQuestion: The survey must have at least one question

guide.title: Climate Survey Guide
guide.whatIsDesc: A climate survey measures employee engagement, satisfaction and well-being. Results help identify strengths and areas for improvement in the organization.
guide.likertDesc: Agreement scale (Strongly disagree to Strongly agree)
guide.npsDesc: Net Promoter Score, measures likelihood of recommending the company
guide.openTextDesc: Free response to capture qualitative feedback
guide.multipleChoiceDesc: Selection of one or more predefined options
guide.flowStep1: Create survey (draft) with questions and configuration
guide.flowStep6: Create development initiatives from the analysis
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: Creates, launches, closes surveys. Views full results and generates AI analysis

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.subtitle: Action history and evidence for your organization
audit.noResultsFiltered: No records found for the selected filters
audit.guideWhatText: The system automatically records every relevant action: approvals, rejections, creations, modifications, evaluation submissions, status changes, feedback, hires, and more. Each record includes who performed the action, when, from which IP, and the details of the change.
audit.guideEvidenceText: Actions marked with the shield icon have evidentiary value: objective approvals/rejections, submitted evaluations, completed check-ins, talent decisions, candidate hires/rejections, and role changes. These actions cannot be retroactively modified or deleted.
audit.guideExportTitle: How to export for legal use?
audit.guideExportText: Use the "Export CSV" button to download a file with all records for the selected date range. The file includes all fields: date, user, email, action, entity, detail, IP, and legal evidence status. The format is compatible with Excel and compliance tools.
audit.guideRetentionText: Audit records are kept indefinitely while the subscription is active. They are not deleted when users are deactivated or evaluation cycles are completed.
audit.guideLaborText: If you need a report for a labor audit or legal process, export the relevant date range with the "Legal evidence only" filter enabled. The resulting CSV contains all the traceability needed: who, what, when, from where, and why.

analyticsPdi.subtitle: Status of individual development plans across the organization

guide.title: Guide: Development Plan Compliance (IDP)
guide.whatDesc: Status of individual development plans — how many plans exist, completion rate, completed vs overdue actions, and breakdown by department.
guide.deptDesc: Shows average progress by area to identify which ones need more support.
guide.exportDesc: Excel and CSV with plan and action details.
guide.permissions: Administrators see the entire organization. Team managers see only their team.

guide.modulesDesc: Which areas of the system are used the most, with translated names.

guide.tenureDesc: Distribution of how long departed employees had been with the organization.

analyticsCiclos.title: Cycle Comparison
analyticsCiclos.subtitle: Select 2 or more cycles to compare and analyze with AI
analyticsCiclos.aiDisclaimer: This analysis consumes 1 AI credit from your monthly quota. Results are generated by artificial intelligence and should be validated by the HR team.
analyticsCiclos.noCycles: No closed cycles available for comparison. Close at least 2 evaluation cycles to see the comparison.
analyticsCiclos.mixedTypesMsg: You have selected cycles of type {{types}}. Scores may differ significantly between evaluation types (a 360° includes more evaluators than a 90°), so the comparison should be interpreted with caution.

guide.title: Guide: Cycle Comparison
guide.whatDesc: Comparison between 2 or more closed evaluation cycles, showing score evolution and distribution by department.
guide.howToDesc: 1) Select 2+ cycles by clicking each one. 2) View automatic comparative charts. 3) Optionally, generate an AI analysis.
guide.chartsDesc: Average/min/max score trend across cycles, and department comparison in a grouped horizontal chart.
guide.aiDesc: Generates an in-depth analysis with executive summary, trends, strengths, alerts, departments that improved/declined, and recommendations. Consumes 1 credit from your monthly AI quota.
guide.biasDesc: Identifies bias patterns in cycle evaluators (leniency, severity, halo effect, central tendency) with statistical evidence and recommendations. Consumes 1 AI credit.
guide.exportDesc: Excel and CSV with data from all cycles and departmental breakdown.
guide.permissions: Administrators see all cycles. Team managers see only their team's data.

crossAnalysis.title: Integrated Performance-Climate Analysis
crossAnalysis.subtitle: Correlation between performance evaluation and workplace climate — one tab per cycle-survey cross.
crossAnalysis.quadrantMapDesc: Each dot is a department. X-axis: performance (0-10), Y-axis: climate (0-10). Thresholds define 4 quadrants.
crossAnalysis.quadrantMapNoData: Not enough departments with performance and climate data to generate the chart. At least 2 departments with both values are required.
crossAnalysis.deptDetailDesc: Performance score (evaluations) and climate (survey) per area, with quadrant classification.
crossAnalysis.climateDimensionsDesc: Average for each climate survey category (scale 1-10).
crossAnalysis.insightsDisclaimer: This analysis is automatically generated by cross-referencing the most recent data. Results should be validated by HR.

quadrants.starAction: Maintain and replicate best practices across other areas
quadrants.burnoutAction: Urgent climate intervention — high turnover risk despite good performance
quadrants.opportunityAction: Invest in technical and management training — the team is motivated
quadrants.criticalAction: Urgent comprehensive action plan — low performance and low engagement

correlationText.strong: There is a strong positive correlation: departments with better climate tend to have better performance. This suggests that well-being initiatives directly impact productivity.
correlationText.moderate: There is a moderate positive correlation: better climate tends to be associated with better performance, although other factors also play a role.
correlationText.weak: The correlation is weak or null: climate and performance appear to be independent in this measurement. This may indicate that teams maintain productivity regardless of satisfaction, or that instruments measure different aspects.
correlationText.negative: There is a negative correlation: some departments with good performance have low climate (possible burnout) and vice versa. This requires immediate attention.

crossAnalysis.noDataMsg: No closed evaluation cycles or surveys are available for analysis.

analysis.title: Quadrant Map Analysis
analysis.deptsAnalyzed: Of {{total}} departments analyzed, {{withData}} have complete performance and climate data.
analysis.perfHighlightText: The highest-scoring department is <best>{{best}}</best> ({{bestScore}}/10) and the lowest is <worst>{{worst}}</worst> ({{worstScore}}/10), a gap of {{gap}} points.
analysis.climaHighlightText: The best climate is in <best>{{best}}</best> ({{bestScore}}/10) and the lowest in <worst>{{worst}}</worst> ({{worstScore}}/10).
analysis.burnoutWarningText: {{depts}} have good performance but deteriorated climate. This may cause turnover of key talent. Recommended: review workloads, implement pulse surveys, and open feedback channels.
analysis.criticalAlertText: {{depts}} require comprehensive intervention. Suggested: deep-dive into root causes, meeting with area leaders, action plan with 30/60/90-day goals.
analysis.opportunityText: {{depts}} have motivated teams with room to improve performance. Recommended: technical training, cross-mentoring with star departments, and clear OKR definition.
analysis.starsText: {{depts}} are organizational benchmarks. Document their best practices and replicate them in other areas. Consider public recognition for the team.
analysis.noQuadrants: No departments classified in quadrants. This may be due to missing climate or performance data for this cross.

results.noAnalysis: Could not load the analysis for this cycle.

comparative.correlationBestWorst: {{best}} has the strongest correlation between performance and climate (r={{bestVal}}), while {{worst}} has the weakest (r={{worstVal}}).
comparative.eNPSBestWorst: The highest eNPS corresponds to {{best}} ({{bestVal}}) and the lowest to {{worst}} ({{worstVal}}).
comparative.consistentCritical: Departments in "Critical" quadrant across all crosses: {{depts}}. Require urgent intervention.
comparative.perfVariability: The average performance difference between crosses is {{diff}} points, indicating variability between periods.

guide.title: Integrated Analysis Guide
guide.whatDesc: Statistical cross-reference between performance evaluations and climate surveys. Identifies patterns not visible when analyzing each system separately.
guide.howDesc: Select 1 climate survey and 1 or more evaluation cycles. A tab will be generated for each cycle-survey cross, plus a global comparative summary.
guide.correlationDesc: Pearson coefficient between climate and performance per department. Values >=0.5 indicate a strong positive relationship.
whyItems: Organizations with better climate achieve up to 21% higher productivity
Enables preventive rather than reactive interventions
Identifies turnover risk in high-performance areas
Aligns development plans with real data
guide.permissions: Administrators see the entire organization. Team managers see only their team.

orgChart.title: Org Chart
orgChart.subtitle: Hierarchical structure of the organization based on manager-employee relationships
orgChart.noFilterResults: No results found with the applied filters

guide.title: Guide: Org Chart
guide.whatDesc: The hierarchical structure in tree form, based on manager-employee relationships.
guide.nodesDesc: Each person displays name, position, department, hierarchy level, and reports.
guide.levelsDesc: Colors and numbers come from the position catalog in Maintenance.
guide.filtersDesc: Search by name or position, and filter by department.
guide.permissions: Administrators and team managers can view the full org chart.

contracts.title: Contracts & Legal Documentation
contracts.subtitle: Contractual documents between your organization and Eva360
contracts.noContracts: No contracts registered for your organization.
contracts.noContractsHint: Contracts will be created by the Ascenda team when the subscription is formalized.

guide.title: Guide: Contracts & Legal Documentation
guide.docsTitle: What documents will you find here?
guide.signatureDesc: Contracts are signed with an OTP code sent to your email. Generates a SHA-256 hash as evidence.
guide.permissions: Administrators can view and sign contracts.
